Okay, welcome back in this lesson. We are going to dive into the second technique for unpacking the situational gestalt and that is deep questioning. So the premise behind this is that if you want to empower your pre-clients, let's not rob them of the opportunity to arrive at their own insights, epiphanies, and breakthroughs. So giving them answers and dropping wisdom bombs is actually a form of what I call performative coaching that may make for great social media clips where everyone applauds how smart and how amazing and how insightful you are. But the problem of doing this on your enrollment calls is that it actually deprives your pre-client from tapping into their own capacities, right? They don't need to see what amazing wisdom bomb dropper genius you are by the end of the call. They need to be connected to their own genius, their own power, their own ability to have their own breakthroughs and realizations and power to act on what is now revealed to them. So this isn't about performing, it is about performing them the service of having their own epiphanies and realizations wherever possible. So even when the diagnosis or reasons why are super clear to you, and oftentimes they will, right? Because you've seen a lot as a coach, as a consultant, you want them to come as close as possible to that conclusion on their own through nothing but your presence and the gift of your questioning. So deep questioning is where you coach them into unpacking the problem and excavating beneath the layers of their own story, beliefs, mental rigidities, and into greater clarity and insight. So these are mostly driven by why questions, right? It could be why do you believe that's true? Why do you feel that this keeps happening? Why do you think that is? Why do you feel your partner is responding to you in that way? Why do you believe staying committed to your workout plan is nearly impossible? Why do you think you're sabotaging your own growth? Why can you tell me more about how that's showing up? Can you give me a bit more clarity around how that looks? Can you share a bit more around how you experience overwhelm in your business? So these are all forms of question that allow someone to go deeper into what has been said to unpack the problem a little bit more fully and to ideally hit this point where they say, oh, wow, maybe things don't have to be that way. Oh, wow, maybe I have actually been acting in a way that is contributing to this problem. Oh, wow, maybe there's this whole other way I can show up in this situation that could totally alter really the success I'm having or the effectiveness I'm having in this area. So these are the types of questions you may ask in a deep questioning context. Just get your prospect a little bit more curious about themselves, a little bit past their own conditioned responses or the limitations they've imposed in the way they've allowed themselves to explore their situations. The reason we do this is people will always value their own answers, insights and epiphanies far more than the ones that are preached to them performatively, right? People want the joy, the elation, the self-congratulatory feeling of arriving at their own epic answer. That's the one they're going to want to invest more action in carrying forward and living through. So in inviting someone to experience liberating epiphanies in your presence, you're actually facilitating all three of those metastates simultaneously, right? There's power in tapping into their own resourcefulness, even if that's just their mental resourcefulness of exploring a situation more fully and deeply than they may have done before, you're connecting them to their vision, right? When they have a liberating epiphany, that's something that they deem to be impossible and stuck and sticky, now actually has new solutions to it that they didn't see before. Through your deep questioning, they get to arrive at that place, all of a sudden their vision is expanded as well, as well as their sense of safety, right? And being able to navigate this situation at their own pace, at their own volition, instead of being preached to, right? If someone is being preached to and essentially being told essentially everything about their life that is going wrong and the things they've done wrong versus actually them realizing it themselves, that's just going to create that closed offness and defense defensiveness, even if what you're saying is accurate and true and real, them arriving at at it through their own perception through their own ability to go deeper into the question is what's going to actually facilitate these three metastates that are going to carry us through towards a positive result on your enrollment calls. As well as all the milestones of part one, right? Resonance with you as a coach or facilitator, because you didn't preach, instead they got to experience something life changing, something liberating, this massive epiphany in your presence that is going to create resonance with you, they're going to feel greater relevance as they arrive at their own insights and diagnostics of their problem that align with your offer. And of course, resourcefulness as they fine tune their ability to shift to change to expand to transform. That's what an epiphany is, it gives the opportunity to opportunity to transform, which is one element of what your offer is likely going to be asking of them. So this is all the stuff that is going on with deep questioning. And my invitation for you is to invite your pre customers, those on your calls to arrive at their own self diagnostics to arrive at their own solutions wherever possible, rather than feeling like your value as a coach is in hitting them over the head with wisdom bombs, right? Yes, there may be a time and place for that. Yes, they may end up in stuckness in their self inquiry in your questioning process. But wherever possible, and for as long as you can actually get them to explore actively, that's where you want to use deep questioning. So use this, use this wisely, use this well, and I will meet you in the next lesson. I'll see you there.
